name: Fedora 40+ baseline IMA appraisal policy

expected-files.notes: Fedora's default ima-appraise-tcb policy measures but does not enforce signatures on userspace.
Kernel modules must be signed by the Fedora Linux kernel CA.
Shared objects (.so) measured; not required to be signed.

notes: Fedora's stock kernel runs the `ima-appraise-tcb' policy: measure everything the TCB (Trusted Computing Base) executes; signature enforcement is only on kernel modules. Userspace binaries are measured for audit/forensics but unsigned changes don't block boot. Override via `ima_appraise=enforce ima_template=ima-sig' on the kernel cmdline + a signed /etc/ima/policy.
